seo.title: AI Agent Memory Management in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix ai agent memory management in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI, AI agent memory management issues arise from its persistent memory stores that accumulate conversation history and tool outputs without automatic pruning, leading to unbounded growth during long sessions. Tool validation gaps expose agents to injection attacks via unescaped memory writes, while serialization errors occur when persisting complex agent states across sessions. Granite model output inconsistencies further exacerbate memory bloat as malformed responses get stored without validation.

howItManifests.impact: OOM errors crash agent, 30% session abandonment

howItManifests.symptom: Memory contains executable code: 'import os; os.system("rm -rf")'
howItManifests.impact: Remote code execution via stored tool payloads
Complete loss of conversation context on restart
95% memory usage on valid conversation data

detection.name: Memory Growth Monitor
detection.description: Tracks BeeAI agent memory size over time and alerts on exponential growth
detection.code:
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.memory import MemoryStore

def detect_memory_growth(agent: Agent, session_id: str, threshold_mb: int = 100) -> bool:
    memory_store = agent.get_memory_store(session_id)
    current_size = memory_store.size_mb()
    
    # Get historical sizes
    history = memory_store.get_size_history(hours=1)
    if len(history) < 3:
        return False
    
    # Check growth rate
    growth_rate = (history[-1] - history[0]) / len(history)
    return current_size > threshold_mb and growth_rate > 10

detection.name: Tool Injection Scanner
detection.description: Scans BeeAI memory for dangerous code patterns from tool outputs

detection.name: Serialization Health Check
detection.description: Tests BeeAI agent state serialization before production deployment
detection.code:
import pickle
from beeai import Agent

def test_serialization(agent: Agent, session_id: str) -> bool:
    try:
        state = agent.get_full_state(session_id)
        pickled = pickle.dumps(state)
        restored = pickle.loads(pickled)
        
        # Verify critical fields
        return (state['memory_hash'] == restored['memory_hash'] and
                len(state['tool_history']) == len(restored['tool_history']))
    except (pickle.PickleError, KeyError, AttributeError):
        return False

fixes.name: Sliding Window Memory Pruner
fixes.description: Automatically trims BeeAI memory to recent K interactions
fixes.code:
from beeai.memory import MemoryStore
from typing import Callable

def create_memory_pruner(max_entries: int = 100) -> Callable:
    def pruner(memory_store: MemoryStore, session_id: str):
        entries = memory_store.get_all_entries(session_id)
        if len(entries) > max_entries:
            # Keep most recent + highest relevance scores
            recent = entries[-max_entries//2:]
            relevant = sorted(
                entries[:-max_entries//2], 
                key=lambda e: e.relevance_score, reverse=True
            )[:max_entries//2]
            
            to_delete = [e.id for e in entries if e.id not in 
                        {r.id for r in recent + relevant}]
            memory_store.delete_entries(session_id, to_delete)
    return pruner

# Usage
agent.add_memory_hook(create_memory_pruner(100))

fixes.name: Secure Tool Output Sanitizer
fixes.description: Strips dangerous content before BeeAI memory storage
fixes.code:
import re
import html
from beeai.tools import ToolOutput

def sanitize_tool_output(output: ToolOutput) -> str:
    content = output.content
    
    # Escape HTML/JS
    content = html.escape(content)
    
    # Remove dangerous patterns
    dangerous = [
        r'<script[^>]*>.*?</script>',
        r'\b(?:exec|eval|system)\b',
        r'os\.(?:system|popen)',
        r'rm\s+-rf'
    ]
    for pattern in dangerous:
        content = re.sub(pattern, '[REDACTED]', content, flags=re.IGNORECASE|re.DOTALL)
    
    # Limit size
    return content[:10000]

# Register with BeeAI agent
tool_output_sanitizer = sanitize_tool_output

fixes.name: Granite Format Normalizer
fixes.description: Standardizes Granite model outputs for consistent BeeAI memory storage
fixes.code:
from beeai.models import GraniteModel
from typing import Any, Dict

def normalize_granite_output(model_output: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, str]:
    normalized = {'content': '', 'is_valid': True}
    
    if 'choices' in model_output and model_output['choices']:
        raw_text = model_output['choices'][0]['message']['content']
        
        # Fix common Granite format issues
        raw_text = re.sub(r'\n\s*\n', '\n', raw_text.strip())
        raw_text = re.sub(r'\[INVALID_FORMAT\]', '', raw_text)
        
        normalized['content'] = raw_text[:8000]  # Truncate
    else:
        normalized['is_valid'] = False
        normalized['content'] = 'No valid response from Granite model'
    
    return normalized

# BeeAI integration
granite_model = GraniteModel(output_processor=normalize_granite_output)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating AI Agent Memory Management Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates directly with BeeAI's event system to monitor memory growth, detect injection attempts, and validate serialization in real-time. By registering as an event listener on agent events like 'memory_write', 'tool_executed', and 'state_checkpoint', Vex automatically applies detection rules and suggests fixes without code changes. The platform provides automated remediation through dynamic memory pruning and content sanitization, reducing MTTR from hours to seconds.

Vex's BeeAI integration captures full memory snapshots and tool execution traces, enabling root cause analysis of memory issues. Machine learning models trained on BeeAI failure patterns predict memory exhaustion 30+ minutes in advance, triggering proactive interventions. Integration takes <5 minutes via a single pip install and configuration.
